trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Cerebrolysin only after hormone optimization is in place: Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:20.880): "cerebrolysin would be the gold standard for for nootropics. But, I don't think you'd have to go that far." Hunter's most recent in-protocol frame (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560): "Cerebrolysin, I would just do 10 to 20 mg per day, 5 days on, 2 days off for 8 weeks" — and for ampules (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:58.000): "like 1 ml to 5 ml three times per week." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh sourcing reality (Hunter email threadId 19c01b888153d27d (2026-01-27): "Gold standard for neural repair — but sourcing is harder (crypto / international friction)") and whether a lighter alternative (P21, Dihexa) is the right fit before committing to a Cerebrolysin cycle.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Sourcing/access friction: Hunter email threadId 19c01b888153d27d (2026-01-27): "Gold standard for neural repair — but sourcing is harder (crypto / international friction)." Hunter directs practitioners to manufacturer prescribing info — Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:04.720): "you can go to this website called cerebrolysin.com" and (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:08.960): "That is the Ever Pharma who is the manufacturer of Cerebrolysin."
- Hormones first, peptide second: Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:17.000): "to be hormone optimization. Yeah. That'll be your the first line of defense." Hunter email threadId 19c01b888153d27d (2026-01-27): "Peptides don't replace hormones (and never will)" and "Women over 60 need hormones FIRST + peptides SECOND."
- Lifestyle floor must be in place: Hunter email threadId 195b0718ac68bbb2 (2025-03-19): "no amount of selank, semax, or cerebrolysin will fully optimize your brain health" without journaling/cognitive offload first.
- Lighter alternatives explicitly offered: Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:24.520): "I think P21 is great. I think Dihexa is great." Hunter email threadId 19c01b888153d27d (2026-01-27): P21 is a "Lighter, more accessible alternative. More selective. Maintenance tool that becomes performance tool when stress rises."
- Acute use case is concussion / TBI / dementia / Alzheimer's: Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14): "Best peptide for concussion recovery? BPC-157, TB-500, and Cerebrolysin." Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:24.560): "if they have ... dementia or Alzheimer's, even multiple sclerosis, all of this stuff, this should be first on your hit parade."
- Trevor's chronic frame is platform-stack, not solo: Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:05.160): "The platform is always based on Tesamorelin, Cerebrolysin, GHK-Cu."
- Stacking note from Hunter: Hunter email threadId 19c49cac85fb70ef (2026-02-10) lists Cerebrolysin among Pinealon's "Stacks Well With" set (Epitalon, Cerebrolysin, Semax, Selank). Hunter's older Dihexa email (threadId 19c01b888153d27d 2026-01-27 referencing Dihexa deep-dive) advises "TBI recovery — Stack with Cerebrolysin + BPC-157 + TB-500."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three converge on Cerebrolysin as the strongest neurotrophic option but reserve it as a heavy-hitter rather than first-line. Hunter email threadId 19c01b888153d27d (2026-01-27): "Gold standard for neural repair." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:20.880): "cerebrolysin would be the gold standard for for nootropics." Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:11.480): "It's a neurotrophic factor concentrate. It's the most powerful agent on the planet for promoting neurogenesis and synaptogenesis and repairing neuronal damage." Trevor (8g4I9x22VNk 00:29:33.960): "this is like the ultimate neurotrophic factor. It repairs damaged neurons. It improves cognitive function. It erases that brain fog." Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:32:13.600): "you want to fix the cognitive aspect? Cerebrolysin" and (xxTZXHynh20 00:32:19.320): "cerebrolysin is a better choice because it's not a synthetic peptide. It's a mixture of neurotrophic factors. It's like giving your brain this incredibly concentrated fertilizer for growth and repair." Same-protocol overlap on the 4/18/26 podcast: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560) and Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:49:49.880) both deliver the identical ask-and-answer for a 55-or-younger no-major-trauma protocol — "do 10 to 20 mg per day, 5 days on, 2 days off for 8 weeks" with the ampule alternative "1 ml to 5 ml three times per week."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Whether Cerebrolysin is needed at all in the absence of trauma. Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:24.510): "But, I don't think you'd have to go that far" and (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:24.520): "I think P21 is great. I think Dihexa is great." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:46.120): "I don't think you need to" stack Cerebrolysin with Cortexin in the same population — but Hunter still gives a full Cerebrolysin protocol when asked. Trevor's framing is more aggressive — chronic platform inclusion: Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:05.160): "The platform is always based on Tesamorelin, Cerebrolysin, GHK-Cu" and "want to play hard? Cerebrolysin" (m8FwL9Xsk78 00:28:44.000): "Crank up the cognitive benefits of the Cerebrolysin. Like, this thing's a freight train of neurotrophic factors." Acute vs chronic emphasis differs by speaker: Trevor's strongest acute use is post-injury cognitive rescue (xxTZXHynh20 00:32:13.600): "you want to fix the cognitive aspect? Cerebrolysin"; Hunter's strongest acute use is concussion recovery (email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df 2025-02-14): "Best peptide for concussion recovery? BPC-157, TB-500, and Cerebrolysin" while his chronic frame is the 8-week 5-on-2-off cycle (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560).

acute.dose: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560): "Cerebrolysin, I would just do 10 to 20 mg per day, 5 days on, 2 days off for 8 weeks." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:58.000) ampule alternative: "like 1 ml to 5 ml three times per week."
acute.route: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:48.360): "if you're using lyophilized Cerebrolysin" implies reconstitution + injection; ampule form is the pharma-supplied IM/SC liquid. No oral route claimed by any expert.
acute.frequency: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:52.800): "5 days on, 2 days off" (lyophilized form). Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:00.840): "three times per week" (ampule form).
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:46.120): "I don't think you need to, but if you're using lyophilized Cerebrolysin, I would just do 10 to 20 mg per day, 5 days on, 2 days off for 8 weeks. If you're getting the ampules of Cerebrolysin, you could do like 1 ml to 5 ml three times per week and get good results." Best-fit candidates per Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14): "Best peptide for concussion recovery? BPC-157, TB-500, and Cerebrolysin." Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:32:13.600): "you want to fix the cognitive aspect? Cerebrolysin."

chronic.dose: Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:05.160): "The platform is always based on Tesamorelin, Cerebrolysin, GHK-Cu" — Trevor does not give a numeric chronic dose for Cerebrolysin in transcript content; his only volunteered numeric reference in this episode is to a different compound ("5 to 10 milligrams every day" of SS-31). Hunter's chronic frame stays the same 10-20 mg / 5-on-2-off / 8 weeks (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560).
chronic.route: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:08.960) directs practitioners to the manufacturer for prescribing info: "That is the Ever Pharma who is the manufacturer of Cerebrolysin" and (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:20.840): "prescribing information for Cerebrolysin which a lot of times is not what the" — Hunter signals the official label is the route-of-administration source.
chronic.frequency: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:52.800): "5 days on, 2 days off" (lyophilized) or (E7GPNidicqY 01:50:00.840): "three times per week" (ampule). Trevor's chronic platform frame is not cycled in his quoted content.
chronic.duration: Hunter cap is the 8-week cycle (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:53.920): "for 8 weeks." Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:29.040): "this should be first on your hit parade. It is a complete systematic update for your CNS, your central nervous system" — implies repeatable but not bounded numerically.
chronic.verbatim_citation: Trevor (m8FwL9Xsk78 00:28:44.000): "want to play hard? Cerebrolysin. Crank up the cognitive benefits of the Cerebrolysin. Like, this thing's a freight train of neurotrophic factors. It's like brain food on steroids." Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:20:05.160): "The platform is always based on Tesamorelin, Cerebrolysin, GHK-Cu." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560): "do 10 to 20 mg per day, 5 days on, 2 days off for 8 weeks."
chronic.timestamp: Trevor 00:20:05.160; Trevor m8FwL9Xsk78 00:28:44.000; Hunter cycling E7GPNidicqY 01:49:51.560

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:49:40.640): "Cerebrolysin in men and women 55 years young, no major brain trauma?
